home.title: BZNS — Micro-Business Launchpad
home.subtitle: Your AI-powered guide to launching a micro-business in Quebec.
home.heroSubtitle: Navigate 6+ government agencies, 80+ funding programs, and a tax system that surprises every first-time entrepreneur — in plain language, in any language.
home.builtForRealSub: Not hypothetical entrepreneurs — actual situations we designed for.
home.readyToLaunchSub: 8 questions. Your personalized Quebec business roadmap. Free.
home.disclaimer: Not legal or financial advice. Always verify with official government sources and qualified professionals.

bento.roadmapDesc: Personalized, dependency-ordered steps. Every form, fee, and deadline for your exact business.
bento.fundingDesc: 15+ Québec programs scored deterministically against your profile. No guesswork.
bento.taxDesc: Real numbers: gross → GST/QST → QPP → income tax → take-home. 2026 rates.
bento.aiDesc: Ask anything about permits, taxes, or Bill 96. Grounded in official KB. Cites sources.
bento.docsDesc: Bilingual contract, invoice template, business pitch — download-ready .docx.

yara.name: Yara, 26
yara.challenge: Wants to sell baked goods from her kitchen but doesn't know where to start legally.

marcus.name: Marcus, 23
marcus.challenge: Thinks he's "just doing side work." About to miss QPP registrations and owe back taxes.

fatima.name: Fatima, 41
fatima.challenge: One of the most regulated micro-businesses in QC — she needs 10 steps she never knew about.
fatima.insight: STA income support program during startup

intake.title: Business Profile
intake.subtitle: Tell us about your business idea to get a personalised roadmap.

q0.title: Your business
q0.subtitle: Let's start with the basics. We'll figure out the right permits, funding, and legal steps for you.
q0.nameLabel: What's the name of your business?
q0.namePlaceholder: e.g., Yara's Kitchen, Studio Nova, QuickFix Reno…
q0.nameHint: Don't have one yet? Just type a working name — you can change it later.
q0.activityLabel: What does your business mainly involve?
q0.foodDesc: Baking, cooking, catering, meal prep, jams, sauces
q0.servicesDesc: Consulting, design, coaching, beauty, fitness, cleaning
q0.professionalDesc: Lawyer, accountant, engineer, architect, therapist, etc.
q0.productsDesc: Online store, boutique, handmade goods, reselling

q1.title: How you'll operate
q1.subtitle: This helps us match the right permits and zoning rules.
q1.homeDesc: Kitchen, office, studio at your residence
q1.clientSitesDesc: You go to the client (renovations, cleaning, etc.)

q2.title: Your pricing & idea
q2.subtitle: How you charge affects tax obligations and financial projections.
q2.pricingLabel: How will you charge your clients?
q2.perItemDesc: Each sale is a fixed price (pastries, crafts, goods)
q2.perSessionDesc: Fixed price per visit (haircut, massage, training)
q2.perProjectDesc: Flat fee per job (photography, design, renovation)
q2.descLabel: Describe your business in a few sentences
q2.descPlaceholder: e.g., I want to sell homemade pastries from my kitchen and deliver in my neighbourhood…
q2.descHint: This helps us generate your personalized legal roadmap. Be as specific or vague as you like.

q3.title: Location & setup
q3.subtitle: Where and how you plan to operate affects which permits and programs apply to you.
q3.cityLabel: Which city or region are you in?
q3.boroughHint: Helps us match borough-specific grants and zoning info.
q3.partnersLabel: Will you have business partners or co-founders?

q4.title: About you
q4.subtitle: Used to match funding programs and services you qualify for. Everything is private and optional.
q4.ageHint: Some grants target entrepreneurs under 35 or over 50.
q4.languagesHint: Select all that apply. We'll surface resources in your languages.
q4.prefLangLabel: Preferred language for your roadmap & AI assistant

roadmap.title: Legal Roadmap
roadmap.subtitle: Personalized, dependency-ordered steps to launch your business in Québec legally.
roadmap.empty: Complete your business profile to generate a personalised roadmap.
roadmap.staleWarning: Your settings changed since this roadmap was generated.
roadmap.reviewBanner: We reviewed your roadmap for your specific business
roadmap.reviewDetail: need your attention before moving forward — tap the highlighted steps to see what to confirm.

stepDetail.notesPlaceholder: e.g., Got my NEQ — number is 1234567890

funding.title: Funding Matches
funding.subtitle: Québec programs you qualify for
funding.empty: Complete your business profile to see funding matches.
funding.pageSubtitle: Grants, loans, and programs matched to your business profile.
funding.findDesc: We'll score every Quebec and federal program against your profile and show you exactly how much you could access right now.
funding.scoringDesc: Matching your profile against all available programs.
funding.noMatchesDesc: Click "Find my funding" above to score programs against your profile.
funding.staleWarning: Your settings changed since your funding was last scored.
funding.readyNowDesc: You meet all current eligibility requirements for these programs.
funding.unlockMore: Unlock with a few more steps
funding.unlockMoreDesc: You meet the core requirements. Complete the flagged steps in your Roadmap to qualify.
funding.roadmapHintNeq: Complete REQ Registration in your Roadmap — it advances your stage from pre-launch to launching, boosting your score for this program.

financial.title: Finances
financial.subtitle: Your financial snapshot
financial.pageSubtitle: Your personal CFO dashboard — estimated taxes, take-home, and what to watch for. Updated live.

section1.title: Your money story
section1.description: Estimated take-home after taxes and expenses, with a quick visual of where each dollar goes.

section2.title: Key numbers
section2.description: Revenue, tax load, expenses, and what you keep — all on one row.

section3.title: Tax breakdown
section3.description: Federal and Québec income tax, QPP, and QPIP — estimated from your net business income (2026 assumptions).

section4.title: Charts & scenarios
section4.description: See how revenue compounds over the year and how different income levels affect your take-home.

section5.title: Expenses & break-even
section5.description: Adjust category amounts to match your reality, then see how close you are to covering costs and taxes.

section6.title: Tax calendar
section6.description: Key dates for installments and filing — use this as a reminder, not personalized advice.

section7.title: Common write-offs
section7.description: Typical deductible costs for your business type — rough savings use a marginal rate estimate.

section8.title: Watch-outs & funding
section8.description: Flags tailored to your numbers, plus how matched funding could extend your runway.

financial.detailedBreakdownSub: Federal + QC income tax, QPP, QPIP — 2026 rates
financial.taxBracketNote: Taxable income = net revenue minus personal amount ({federal} federal, {qc} QC)

projection.title: 12-Month Projection
projection.gstCross: You'll cross the $30,000 GST/QST threshold in month {month} — register before then.
projection.gstSafe: At this revenue level you stay under the $30,000 threshold — no GST/QST registration required this year.

scenarios.title: Revenue scenarios
scenarios.subtitle: What if you earn more or less than expected?
scenarios.basedOn: Based on 70% / 100% / 140% of your expected {amount}/month revenue

breakEven.title: Break-Even
breakEven.subtitle: Minimum revenue to cover expenses + all taxes
breakEven.growthMsg: At 10% monthly growth, you could reach break-even in ~{months} months. Focus on building revenue above {target}/mo.
breakEven.belowMsg: You're {amount}/mo below break-even. Consider reducing expenses or increasing prices.

pricing.chargingMore: Charging {price} instead adds {amount}/month to your take-home (approx).

calendar.title: 2026 Tax Calendar
calendar.note: * Installments required when total tax > $1,800/year. Self-employed filing deadline is June 15, but any balance owed is due April 30.

qpp.title: The QPP Double Contribution
qpp.desc: As a self-employed person, you pay both the employee AND employer share of QPP. An employee at the same income would pay only {employee}/mo — you pay {selfEmployed}/mo.
qpp.goodNews: The good news: half of your QPP is tax-deductible, reducing your taxable income.

deductions.title: Deduction tracker
deductions.subtitle: Pre-loaded for your business type
deductions.fromDeductions: from {amount} in deductions at ~{rate} marginal rate

fundingImpact.title: Funding Impact
fundingImpact.subtitle: How matched funding extends your runway
fundingImpact.basedOn: Based on your matched funding programs

flags.gstApproachingDetail: You're {remaining} away from the $30,000 registration threshold. Plan to register before you cross it to avoid penalties.
flags.gstRequiredDetail: At ${revenueK}K/year, you must register for GST/QST within 30 days of crossing the threshold and start collecting sales tax.
flags.quarterlyInstallmentsDetail: Your estimated tax ({totalTax}/yr) exceeds $1,800 — you must pay {installment} quarterly starting March 15. Set this aside monthly.
flags.qppBudget: Budget for QPP — most first-timers miss this
flags.qppBudgetDetail: QPP costs self-employed people 12.8% of net income (both shares). That's {qppMonthly}/month you need to set aside — it's not deducted automatically.
flags.mileageTipDetail: Driving to farmers markets, ingredient shopping, and deliveries is deductible. At the 2026 rate of $0.72/km, 200km/month = $144 in deductions — about $36 in tax savings.
flags.homeOfficeTipDetail: If you work from home, you can deduct a proportional share of rent, utilities, and internet. A 10m² office in a 100m² home = 10% of housing costs deductible.
flags.staTipDetail: The Service de garde en milieu familial STA program provides income support while you build up your enrollment. Apply through your RCE before opening.
flags.belowBreakEvenDetail: At this revenue level your take-home ({takeHome}/mo) doesn't cover your expenses ({expenses}/mo). You need at least {needed} gross/mo to break even.

assistant.greeting: Hey! How can I help you today?
assistant.title: BZNS Assistant
assistant.emptyState: Ask me anything about starting your business in Quebec!
assistant.suggestion1: How do I register a sole proprietorship in Quebec?
assistant.suggestion2: Do I need to collect GST/QST?
assistant.suggestion3: What funding is available for women entrepreneurs?

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your profile and business information.
settings.roadmapNotice: After saving changes to your business type or location, go to your dashboard and click {bold} to get updated steps.
settings.emailHint: Your login email — managed through magic link authentication.
settings.businessTypeHint: Used to select the right legal steps and funding programs for your roadmap.
settings.businessNameHint: Leave blank if you haven't chosen a name yet.
settings.businessDescriptionHint: A short summary of what you do. Used to personalize your roadmap.
settings.boroughHint: Montréal borough, if applicable (e.g. Villeray, Plateau-Mont-Royal).
settings.expectedRevenueHint: Used to calculate your tax snapshot and GST/QST registration threshold.

dashboard.staleWarning: Your settings changed since this roadmap was generated.
dashboard.staleDetail: Your settings changed since your roadmap was generated. Regenerate to get updated steps.
dashboard.generateDesc: Generate a personalized legal checklist for your business.
